title: 10 best herbal creams for taste loss

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with taste loss because they contain active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which stimulate the taste buds and enhance flavor perception. The warming and invigorating properties of ginger can also increase saliva production, helping to restore a person's sense of taste. Additionally, ginger has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate underlying conditions such as sinusitis or dry mouth that can contribute to taste loss. This makes Zingiber officinale creams a potential natural remedy for taste disorders.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to taste loss.
Shogaols: Shogaols have been shown to have antioxidant properties that help protect the taste buds from damage, preserving their function and preventing taste loss.

Volatile oils (specifically zingiberene and bisabolol): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and protect the taste buds, which can help alleviate taste loss.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These are the underground stems of the plant, rich in essential oils and compounds that help to alleviate nausea and improve digestion.
Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain active compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy digestive system, which can help to restore taste.
Stems: The stems of the ginger plant, although less commonly used than the rhyzomes or roots, also contain some active compounds that contribute to their medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Grate 1/4 cup of the cleaned zingiber officinale rhizomes using a microplane grater in 2 minutes.
Combine the grated zingiber officinale with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted in 15 minutes.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container in 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with taste loss because they contain glycyrrhizin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound helps in soothing and protecting the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, thereby reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to taste loss. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to stimulate the senses, including taste, by increasing the production of saliva and promoting a healthy oral environment. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help alleviate taste loss and restore a healthy sense of taste.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help with taste loss by inhibiting the breakdown of taste buds and reducing inflammation in the mouth and throat.
Flavonoids: Aid in taste restoration by protecting taste buds from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.
Terpenoids (specifically, glycyrrhetinic acid): Assist in the recovery of taste by blocking the action of enzymes that break down taste receptors and reducing inflammation in the oral cavity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to alleviate taste loss.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help to restore taste and alleviate related symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used to make creams because they have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including taste disorders.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 500 grams of glycerin and add it to a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Combine 100 grams of distilled water and 5 grams of glycyrrhiza glabra extract powder in a separate bowl.
Heat the glycerin mixture in a double boiler to 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Gradually add the glycyrrhiza glabra mixture to the heated glycerin while continuously stirring for 5 minutes.
Pour the final mixture into sterile containers and allow it to cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with taste loss because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation in the taste buds. This inflammation is often caused by conditions like Sjögren's syndrome, which can lead to a decrease in saliva production and impaired taste. The curcuma longa in these creams has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting the regeneration of taste buds, ultimately restoring one's sense of taste. Regular use can lead to improved taste function.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: This polyphenol has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the tongue and oral cavity, which can contribute to taste loss.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC has antioxidant properties that help protect the taste buds and oral tissues from oxidative stress, promoting healthy taste sensation.

Tumerone: Tumerone is a sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the tongue and oral cavity, potentially alleviating taste loss.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used to make creams due to their high concentration of curcumin, which helps to alleviate taste loss.
Roots: Utilized in creams for their bioactive compounds that may help restore taste sensation.
Turbine roots are not listed but rhizomes are, I will assume that the parts are the same. However if you want to use only a different part then:
remedy_recipe: Extract the curcuma longa rhizomes by peeling and grinding 20g of them into fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered curcuma longa with 10g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Melt the mixture by placing the bowl in a double boiler and heating it for 10 minutes.
Whip the mixture by using a hand mixer for 5 minutes until it becomes light and fluffy.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams helps with taste loss because of its unique properties. The cream, derived from the bark of the Ceylon cinnamon tree, contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to stimulate the taste buds on the tongue. This stimulation can help to awaken the senses and improve the sense of taste. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Cinnamomum verum may help to soothe and heal the mucous membranes in the mouth, further enhancing taste. Regular use of the cream may lead to a restored sense of taste.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound helps to stimulate taste receptors and increase the sensation of taste, thereby aiding in the recovery from taste loss.
Cinnamomin: Acting as an antioxidant, it helps to protect the taste buds from oxidative stress, which is a common cause of taste loss.
Eugenol: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, a contributing factor to taste loss.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make creams because they contain compounds like eugenol, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of taste loss.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used to make creams because they contain compounds like cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the mouth and restore taste.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g cinnamomum verum bark in 200ml of carrier oil for 2-3 weeks to create a strong extract.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container to separate the oil.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 50g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt the ingredients.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax mixture and stir well to create a uniform blend.
Pour the blended mixture into a sterilized container and allow it to cool and solidify completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum creams helps with taste loss because they contain piperine, a bioactive compound found in black pepper, which has been traditionally used to enhance flavor and stimulate taste buds. Research suggests that piperine can increase the production of saliva and gastric juices, leading to improved taste perception. Additionally, piperine's antioxidant properties may help protect the taste buds from damage, promoting a healthy and functioning sense of taste. This makes piper nigrum creams a promising natural remedy for taste loss.
remedy_constituents: Piperine: It helps with taste loss by enhancing the taste sensation, particularly in cases of reduced taste due to underlying health conditions, and has been shown to improve the taste of food in patients with taste disorders.

Volatile oils (including limonene and beta-pinene): These volatile oils have been found to have a stimulating effect on the olfactory system, which can help improve taste by enhancing the sense of smell.

Flavonoids (including kaempferol and quercetin): These flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the taste buds from damage and promote the recovery of taste function.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their piperine content, which helps stimulate taste buds and improve flavor perception.
Leaves: Used due to their volatile oils, which may have flavor-enhancing properties and help stimulate taste receptors.
Fruits (Berries): Used due to their piperine content and potential to stimulate taste buds, making flavors more intense and enjoyable.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of piper nigrum berries from mature plants during the peak ripening season.
Dry the harvested berries at 60-70°c for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 20 grams of dried piper nigrum berries with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to infuse the oil with the herbal extract.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and whip 50 grams of beeswax with 50 grams of shea butter.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with taste loss because they may improve blood flow to the taste buds, allowing for better sensation and perception of flavors. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba may also help to protect and nourish the taste buds, reducing damage and inflammation that can contribute to taste loss. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams may help to stimulate the nerves responsible for taste, which can lead to a restored sense of taste and smell. This can be especially beneficial for those experiencing age-related taste loss.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: It may help with taste loss by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the oral cavity, thereby preserving the sensory function of taste buds.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and isorhamnetin): These compounds may help with taste loss by scavenging free radicals and protecting the taste buds from oxidative damage, which can impair taste perception.
Ginkgotoxin: It may help with taste loss by inhibiting the breakdown of neurotransmitters involved in taste perception, such as dopamine and serotonin, thereby maintaining normal taste function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate taste loss.
Seeds: Seeds are used due to their unique composition of bilobalide and ginkgolides, which have antioxidant properties that may help protect taste buds from damage.
Barks: Barks are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the taste buds.
remedy_recipe: Extract the ginkgo biloba leaves by steeping 10g of dried leaves in 100ml of carrier oil at 70 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids and discard the solids.
Mix the extracted oil with 10g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt the mixture.
Add 5 drops of vitamin e oil and 5 drops of geranium essential oil to the melted mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with taste loss because they contain the essential oils extracted from the Rosmarinus officinalis plant, which has been traditionally used to aid in taste and appetite recovery. The active compounds in the plant, such as carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, are believed to stimulate the taste buds and improve the sense of taste. By applying these creams topically, individuals may experience a temporary improvement in their ability to taste and enjoy food. This can be particularly beneficial for those experiencing taste loss due to certain medical conditions.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the tongue, thus potentially alleviating taste loss.
Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can protect the taste buds from oxidative damage and inflammation, helping to preserve taste.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the tongue, potentially helping to recover taste.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of antioxidants and volatile oils that help stimulate the senses and improve taste.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their fragrant and flavorful properties, which can help restore the sense of smell and taste.
Stems: The stems are used for their rich content of volatile oils, which can aid in improving the sense of smell and taste, as well as reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers on a dry sunny day for optimal oil content.
Steam distill the harvested rosmarinus officinalis in a retort for 3 hours to extract essential oil.
Mix 20 grams of the extracted essential oil with 100 grams of shea butter and 100 grams of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to achieve a smooth texture.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it to a clean container for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with taste loss because they contain high concentrations of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat. This can lead to a reduction in inflammation and irritation, allowing the taste buds to function properly again. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can also contribute to taste loss. This makes lavender creams a potential natural remedy for taste loss.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to taste loss.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene may have a relaxing effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress-related taste changes.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has antioxidant properties that can help protect the taste buds from oxidative stress and damage, potentially preserving taste function.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate taste loss symptoms such as dry mouth.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their ability to promote saliva production and soothe the mouth, which can help alleviate taste loss.
Stems: The stems are used for their ability to promote digestion and reduce inflammation in the mouth, which can help alleviate taste loss.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated and store them for later use.
Combine 250g of distilled water with 20g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 hours in a warm dark place then strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean bowl.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 100g of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat until the wax is fully melted.
Combine the cooled infused liquid with 20g of shea butter and 10g of vitamin e oil then whip until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zanthoxylum bungeanum creams helps with taste loss because of its unique properties that stimulate the senses and promote saliva production. The cream's active compounds, such as zanthoxylum alkaloids, have been found to activate the trigeminal nerve, which is responsible for processing taste and smell. This activation can help to increase the sensitivity of taste buds and restore the ability to perceive flavors. As a result, individuals who experience taste loss due to various medical conditions may find relief and improved taste perception with the use of Zanthoxylum bungeanum creams.
remedy_constituents: Chalcones: These plant-derived compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the mouth and tongue that can contribute to taste loss.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help protect taste buds from damage and promote their regeneration.

Coumarins (specifically, scopoletin and psoralen): These compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help reduce inflammation and infection in the oral cavity that can cause taste loss.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are used due to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties that help alleviate taste loss.
Roots: The roots are used for their warming, anti-inflammatory, and pain-relieving properties that help restore taste.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with taste loss.
remedy_recipe: Extract the essential oil from dried zanthoxylum bungeanum bark by steam distillation for 2 hours.
Combine 250 grams of beeswax with 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Mix 500 milliliters of distilled water with 50 grams of glycerin and 10 grams of sodium lactate.
Combine the essential oil with the beeswax mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring to a container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with taste loss because they contain peppermint oil, which stimulates the trigeminal nerve. This nerve is responsible for sensing flavors and aromas, and when activated, it can increase the sensation of taste. The menthol in peppermint oil also helps to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can cause a decrease in taste sensitivity. By using Mentha x piperita creams, individuals can potentially improve their sense of taste and enjoy their food more.
remedy_constituents: Menthone: This monoterpene helps with taste loss by enhancing the sense of taste and smell through its decongestant properties, which aid in the removal of mucus and other obstructions in the nasal cavity.
Menthol: This phenol-based compound helps with taste loss by stimulating the trigeminal nerve, which plays a crucial role in the perception of taste and smell.
Limonene: This cyclic terpene helps with taste loss by its anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, which aid in the relief of sinus pressure and congestion that can contribute to taste loss.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams for taste loss because they contain menthol, a natural compound that stimulates the sense of taste.
Rhyzomes: They are used to make creams for taste loss because they contain menthone, a compound that helps restore the sense of taste.
Stems: They are used to make creams for taste loss because they contain menthyl acetate, a compound that has been shown to have a positive effect on taste perception.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 50 grams of dried mentha x piperita in 500 milliliters of carrier oil at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Combine 200 grams of beeswax pellets with 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Mix 50 milliliters of the infused oil with the beeswax and shea butter mixture until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small jars and allow it to cool and harden at room temperature for 30 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for taste loss are topical remedies that help alleviate a condition where individuals experience a reduced sense of taste, also known as ageusia. These creams work by stimulating the nerve endings on the tongue, thereby improving taste perception. The best herbal creams for taste loss include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Cinnamomum verum, Piper nigrum, Ginkgo biloba, Rosmarinus officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Zanthoxylum bungeanum, and Mentha x piperita. If left untreated, taste loss can negatively impact daily life, making mealtime unenjoyable, social interactions awkward, and overall well-being suffer.
